paper_title: The Kromatic Symmetric Function: A $K$-theoretic Analogue of $X_G$
summary: No follow-up work addressing the Grassmannian subvariety construction problem has been found. Subsequent papers on the Kromatic symmetric function (2024–2025) focus on power-sum expansions and graph-distinguishing properties, leaving the geometric question — an explicit construction of Grassmannian subvarieties whose cohomology class is $X_G$ and K-theoretic structure sheaf class is $\overline{X}_G$ for each claw-free incomparability graph — entirely open.
search_queries: Kromatic symmetric function Grassmannian subvariety claw-free incomparability graph K-theory
Crew Pechenik Spirkl Kromatic symmetric function 2301.02177 follow-up
Grassmannian subvariety cohomology K-theory claw-free incomparability graph chromatic symmetric function Grothendieck
notes: Three follow-up papers were found on Kromatic symmetric functions (arXiv:2408.01395, 2502.21285, 2508.17682), but all address algebraic properties (power-sum expansions, graph distinguishing) rather than the geometric Grassmannian construction. The conjecture is stated informally as a geometric desideratum motivated by the $\overline{s}$-positivity result; no follow-up on the geometric side was found in the indexed literature.
